channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Anthropic was supposed to be a ‘safe’ alternative to OpenAI, but CEO Dario Amodei admits his company struggles to balance safety with profits
item.description: Anthropic’s CEO says intense commercial pressure is testing the company’s safety-first AI mission.

item.title: Perú destituye al presidente, otra vez
item.description: El Congreso peruano aprobó la destitución del presidente José Jerí. El mandatario tenía cuatro meses en el cargo, que asumió después de que Dina Bolua...

item.title: Companies are cycling through CEOs—and replacing them with first-timers
item.description: The year of the rookie CEO has arrived.

item.title: Thousands of CEOs just admitted AI had no impact on employment or productivity—and it has economists resurrecting a paradox from 40 years ago
item.description: In the 1980s, economist Robert Solow made an observation that reminded economists of today’s AI boom: “You can see the computer age everywhere but in ...

item.title: Ray Dalio warns of ‘great disorder’ period for world economy, marked by ‘clash of great powers’—just like the 1930s
item.description: The billionaire hedge fund manager warned that the world has entered a historical cycle that he believes is the most turbulent and violent possible.

item.title: The Price of Admission to Epstein’s World: Silence
item.description: There were plenty of signs that something wasn’t right with Jeffrey Epstein. Why didn’t anyone say something?

item.title: U.S. and Iran Make ‘Good Progress’ in Geneva Talks, Foreign Minister Says
item.description: The Iranian minister, Abbas Araghchi, said both sides had agreed on a “set of guiding principles.” President Trump has called on Iran to make an immed...

item.title: Colbert Slams Trump Administration After CBS Pulls Talarico Interview
item.description: Stephen Colbert said he had to drop an interview with James Talarico from his Monday broadcast because of new F.C.C. guidance that targeted political ...

item.title: $56 trillion national debt leading to a spiraling crisis: Budget watchdog warns the U.S. is walking a crumbling path
item.description: Trump is putting the U.S. economy on a "sugar high" and leaving other people holding the bill when it comes due a decade from now, according to the CR...

item.title: Questions Swirl Around Russian Figure Skater in Her Olympic Debut
item.description: Adeliia Petrosian, 18, has the résumé of a medal contender — and ties to coaches and a skater who were at the center of a doping scandal at the last W...

item.title: Peru Votes to Impeach President José Jerí
item.description: Peru’s Congress voted to impeach President José Jerí, four months after he replaced Dina Boluarte, who had also been removed from the presidency.

item.title: California Forecast: Storms to Bring More Snow and Rain
item.description: From flood warnings around Los Angeles to heavy snow in the Sierra, the storm systems are an abrupt shift from recent dry weather.

item.title: Palmerston, the Former ‘Chief Mouser’ for Britain’s Foreign Office, Dies
item.description: The cat, who was named after a former prime minister, became a social media star. By the time of his death at about 12, he had more followers than man...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Make ‘Good Progress’ in Geneva Talks, Foreign Minister Says
item.description: The Iranian minister, Abbas Araghchi, said both sides had agreed on a “set of guiding principles.” President Trump has called on Iran to make an immed...

item.title: High-Stakes Nuclear Talks Between U.S. and Iran Set to Kick Off
item.description: The United States and Iran have been flexing their military might as their negotiators resume nuclear talks in Switzerland.
